RESOLUTION #117 
CONFLICT OF INTEREST 
WHEREAS. public school boards are required by public law (Government Code 
Sections 87100 et seq. ) to prepare and adopt a district Conflict of Interest 
Code. and 
WHEREAS. board members of said school boards are required by law to file a 
disclosure statement disclosing their interest and those of their immediate 
families in investments, real property. and income. and 
WHEREAS. these disclosure statements are by law made available for public 
scrutiny. and 
WHEREAS. other sections of the Government Code (Section 1090 et seq.) and 
Title 2. California Administrative Code (Section 18700 et seq. ) and the Ed-
ucation Code (Section 1174) provide the needed legal control of board member 
actions. and 
WHEREAS. the California "Political Reform Act ~f 1974" is an infringement on 
the rights of private citizens and their immediate families and is an invasion 
of personal privacy of board members and their immediate families. and 
WHEREAS. this act can require personal expenses for attorney and accountant 
fees by board members holding non-paying elective board positions, and 
WHEREAS. compliance with the requirements of this Code will encourage private 
investigative snooping by persons bent on personal vendettas. and 
WHEREAS, 'compliance with the requirements of the California "Political Reform-
Act of 1974" by boards will result in the resignation of untold numbers of board 
members throughout the state, and 
WHEREAS. school districts and communities of the State need citizens with financial 
solvency. business acumen. and personal stability to serve as board members. 
IT IS HEREBY RESOLVED by the Board of Education of the River Delta Unified School 
District to publicly state its opposition to the inclusion of elected public school board 
members within the requirments of the California "Political Reform Act of 1974" and 
it is resolved to urge its legislators to take the necessary action to bring about the 
changes needed to exciude school board members from the requirements of the Califor 
nia "Political Reform Act of 1974". 
AYES: 
NOES: 
Hamilton. Lawrence, Lewallen, Merwin. Silva. Upham 
None 
ABSENT: Himoto 
I hereby certify that the foregoing is a true and correct transcript of a resolution duly 
adopted and affirmed by a formal vote of the members of the Gove 
ing Board of the. 
River Delta Unified School District at a duly cq,bjtituted regula meeting of the Board

sections.question_synthetic: This document is not a legal question but rather a formal resolution by the River Delta Unified School District Board of Education opposing the application of the California Political Reform Act of 1974 to elected school board members.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a formal resolution, not an FPPC advice letter or incoming request. Contains no QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. The document expresses opposition to FPPC requirements rather than seeking guidance. Confidence slightly reduced due to image quality and partial illegibility of signature/attestation section.

embedding.summary: This is Resolution #117 adopted by the River Delta Unified School District Board of Education on August 3, 1976, formally opposing the inclusion of elected public school board members in the requirements of the California Political Reform Act of 1974 and urging legislators to exclude school boards from the Act's disclosure and conflict of interest requirements.
